feuds:
Kyle Richards: tension over Erika's narrative control and alleged knowledge of Tom's misconduct
Sutton Stracke: confrontation over Erika calling Sutton a liar and Sutton pushing back on Tom Girardi's victims
Garcelle Beauvais: underlying tension over Erika's credibility and deflection
Lisa Rinna: surface alliance but scrutiny over Erika's handling of the situation
key_moments:
Challenged on whether she knew about Tom Girardi's fraudulent activities: 'I can't control what Tom Girardi did'
Pressed on why she didn't leave Tom sooner: 'Where was I going?'
Confronted about $20 million deposited into her account while Tom had affairs: 'He put 20 million dollars and more into your account'
Asked directly if she asked Tom whether he did it, responded: 'I asked him' — followed by emotional music sting
Challenged over manipulating the narrative about Tom's cognitive decline timeline: 'You were manipulating the narrative by saying that the cognitive decline started three years ago — that's not true'
Defiant declaration: 'The moment you call me a liar it's game on'
talking_points:
Maintain that you had no knowledge of Tom's financial crimes and could not control his actions
Address the $20 million account question head-on — frame it as marital finances, not complicity
Push back on the cognitive decline timeline allegation — clarify your public statements were not manipulative
Acknowledge the victims' pain while defending your own position as also a victim of Tom's deception
Be prepared for the 'why didn't you leave sooner?' question — have a clear, emotionally grounded answer ready
confessional_draft: Everyone keeps asking me what I knew and when I knew it — but nobody wants to hear the truth, they just want a villain. I did not know what Tom was doing, and no amount of bullying from these women is going to make me confess to something I didn't do. The moment you call me a liar, it's game on — and honey, I've been in the game a long time.

feuds:
Erika Girardi: skepticism over Erika's story and narrative control
Kathy Hilton: subtle family tension underlying the reunion dynamic
key_moments:
Referenced a private phone call to one of the women: 'Remember when you called me and you said these girls have flipped on you — well, that'll come up for the reunion, get ready'
Positioned as a key information broker between cast members regarding shifting alliances
talking_points:
Own your role as the messenger — you were relaying information, not stirring the pot
Clarify where you stand on Erika's situation: sympathetic but skeptical
Address the phone call allegation directly and explain the context
Navigate the Kathy dynamic carefully — she's your sister, but you're also an independent voice on this show
Position yourself as the moral compass of the group without coming across as self-righteous
confessional_draft: I've been on this show long enough to know when someone is working a room, and I'd be lying if I said I didn't have questions about Erika's story. I care about her as a friend, but I also care about the truth. And yes, I made that phone call — because that's what friends do, they give you a heads up.

feuds:
Garcelle Beauvais: called out for her behavior toward Garcelle and perceived attacks
Sutton Stracke: ongoing friction and confrontational dynamic throughout the season
Crystal Kung Minkoff: tension stemming from season conflicts
key_moments:
Fiery pre-reunion declaration: 'There's gonna be some shots taken and some shots fired'
Warned the cast: 'There's gonna be some winners and there's gonna be some losers — well, that's life baby'
Challenged by another Housewife who asks why she didn't confront her directly: 'Why don't you come to me and say Rinna, what are you doing?'
Confronted about her anger level: 'Am I angry? Now enough for you?'
talking_points:
Own your aggressive energy this season — frame it as passion, not cruelty
Be ready to explain why you defended Erika so hard while others had doubts
Address the 'shots fired' mentality — are you the aggressor or the defender?
Have a response ready for those who say you attack first and ask questions later
Acknowledge if your loyalty to Erika clouded your judgment toward other cast members
confessional_draft: I said what I said — there are gonna be winners and losers at this reunion, and I did not come here to lose. People think I'm the villain this season, but I was the only one willing to stand up and say what everyone else was whispering. That's not cruelty, that's called being real.

feuds:
Sutton Stracke: ongoing friction over perceived judgment and social dynamics
Garcelle Beauvais: tension over tone and communication style
key_moments:
Called out over the way she spoke to another castmate: 'The way that you spoke to me is not okay'
Accused of laughing at another woman's expense: 'You and your husband were laughing at my supplies'
talking_points:
Address the accusation about laughing at a castmate — clarify the context and apologize if needed
Acknowledge the communication style criticism and show growth
Reinforce your bond with Kyle as a stable alliance in a chaotic season
Be prepared to defend PK's behavior at social events if it comes up again
Highlight your own emotional moments this season to show vulnerability, not just polish
confessional_draft: I would never intentionally mock or belittle someone, and the fact that my intentions are being twisted into something ugly is genuinely hurtful. PK and I were not laughing at anyone — and if it came across that way, I am truly sorry. But let's be honest, there are bigger things to address at this reunion than a misunderstood moment.

feuds:
Erika Girardi: consistently questioned Erika's honesty and held her accountable for the victims
Lisa Rinna: called out Rinna's combative behavior and perceived bullying tactics
key_moments:
Pressed Erika on the air crash victims: 'It lands differently when there are air crash victims'
Demanded Erika ask Tom directly about his guilt: 'Have you asked him if he did it?'
Challenged Erika to actually back up her claims, asking others: 'Can someone please back me the [expletive] up on what I'm saying?'
talking_points:
Stand firmly as the voice of accountability for Erika's victims — do not back down
Be ready for pushback from Rinna loyalists — stay calm and factual
Articulate clearly why the air crash victims framing is so important to you personally
Address your evolving friendship with Sutton and how you became aligned this season
Prepare for questions about whether you were too hard on Erika or appropriately tough
confessional_draft: I came into this franchise to bring a different perspective, and part of that is not looking the other way when real people are hurting. Those air crash victims are not a storyline — they are real people who lost everything. Somebody on this couch had to say it out loud, and I'm glad it was me.

feuds:
Erika Girardi: direct and repeated confrontations over Erika's credibility and the victims
Lisa Rinna: Rinna's aggressive behavior toward Sutton throughout the season
Crystal Kung Minkoff: tension around boundaries and the infamous bedroom incident
key_moments:
Erika threatened her directly: 'We're gonna put you on a skewer and I'm gonna fire up the barbecue'
Held firm against Erika's intimidation tactics around the Tom Girardi fraud narrative
Part of the broader group confronting Erika about manipulating the cognitive decline timeline
talking_points:
You stood your ground against Erika's intimidation — own that courage
Address the 'skewer' threat directly and explain how it made you feel
Clarify your position on Tom's victims: it was never personal against Erika, it was about accountability
Be ready to revisit the Crystal bedroom conflict and show where you two stand now
Defend your consistency — you said the same things publicly that you said privately
confessional_draft: When someone tells you they're going to put you on a skewer, you have two choices: run, or stand your ground. I chose to stand my ground. I wasn't trying to destroy Erika — I was trying to make sure we didn't all just look away from the people who actually got hurt.

feuds:
Sutton Stracke: the season-defining bedroom/boundaries conflict
Lisa Rinna: caught in crossfire of broader cast tensions
key_moments:
Referenced indirectly in the broader reunion tension as a newer cast member navigating established dynamics
The bedroom incident with Sutton looms as a defining talking point for her first reunion appearance
talking_points:
Address the Sutton bedroom incident with clarity — what your boundaries were and why they mattered
Establish yourself as a confident new voice, not just a reactive player
Be ready to explain how you navigated being a rookie in a cast full of veterans
Share how the season's heavy Erika/Tom storyline affected you as a newer Housewife
Show that you've grown from the conflicts of the season and have a clear perspective going forward
confessional_draft: Being new to this group means everyone is watching to see if you'll fold under pressure — and I refuse to fold. What happened between Sutton and me was real, my feelings were valid, and I'm not going to minimize that just to keep the peace. I know who I am, and this reunion is where I get to prove it.

feuds:
General cast tension: arrived with prepared notes to address multiple grievances
Dorit Kemsley / castmates: the 'laughing at supplies' confrontation
key_moments:
Made a notable entrance mid-reunion: 'We are joined by the one and only Kathy Hilton'
Arrived with literal written notes: 'I have a few notes in case because I wanted to address a few things'
Took accountability for her actions: 'All these things I don't feel great about — I did them, it happened, each and every one of them haunts me'
Revealed the scale of hate speech she received: 'It's hundreds a day'
Called out castmates for laughing: 'You and your husband were laughing at my supplies'
talking_points:
Your accountability moment — owning your mistakes — is your strongest card, play it early
Address the online hate speech you've received and the emotional toll it took
Explain the notes: you came prepared because you wanted to be precise, not because you were coached
Clarify the 'supplies' incident and why it felt like a betrayal from people you trusted
Navigate the Kyle family dynamic carefully — sisterhood is complicated but it's real
confessional_draft: I came to this reunion with my notes and my truth, because I owed it to everyone — including myself — to say what I needed to say clearly. Yes, I made mistakes, and I own every single one of them. But receiving hundreds of hateful messages a day because of how this season was edited? That's something I needed people to hear.
